scenario: Vanta Bank runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 22000 transactions per day. The operator is migrating 450 active instances of paymentProcess from version 4 to version 5 and now needs to indicate which target flow node each source service task should map to in the migration view of Operate on the cluster.
question: How does the operator declare which target flow node each source flow node maps to in the migration view?

options.text: Drag each source flow node onto a target flow node directly in the diagrams; the migration view then records the mapping in the bottom panel automatically for the operation
Type a FEEL expression in the mapping panel that resolves to the target flow node id at runtime when the migration operation is started on the affected cluster
Click each source flow node in the diagram; the migration view then auto-maps it to a target flow node with a matching BPMN id without any operator action being needed
Use the dropdowns in the bottom flow node mapping panel to pick a target flow node for each source flow node that should be part of the migration plan in Operate

a.text: Incorrect. The migration view does not record mappings via drag-and-drop on the diagrams; the documented mechanism is the dropdowns in the bottom flow node mapping panel for each source.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no FEEL-based runtime resolution of target flow nodes during migration; the operator uses the dropdowns in the bottom mapping panel to choose each target explicitly.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no auto-map-on-click behavior in the migration view; each source flow node is mapped explicitly via the dropdowns in the bottom flow node mapping panel.

d.text: Correct. The migration guide says the operator uses the dropdowns in the bottom flow node mapping panel to select a target flow node for each source flow node that should be part of the migration.

explanation: In the migration view, the bottom flow node mapping panel lists each source flow node and exposes dropdowns; the operator selects, for each source flow node that should be part of the migration, the matching target flow node.
